scope: hero.archer runtime integration (Neutral/Idle/Move/Attack v3.2) into the actual game runtime, actual-board evidence, x1 + x4 validation

PR81.role: 16-bit compatibility check, base of this runtime-integration branch
PR81.head: 5a42b921f3371bfdaf1c5f22e5bbf750b296818d

assetHashPreservation.method: SHA-256 re-hash of every protected binary, before and after all runtime code changes

attackV3_2Frame011ExactNeutralMasterMatc.note: exact match to Neutral Master, as approved

assetHashPreservation.attackV3_2AllTwelveFrames: all 12 frames (000-011) re-hashed and confirmed byte-identical to the approved package on record
assetHashPreservation.idlePackageEightFrames: all 8 frames re-hashed and confirmed byte-identical against data/design/archer-idle-package-exact-file-approval-v1.json
assetHashPreservation.movePackageEightFrames: all 8 frames re-hashed and confirmed byte-identical against data/design/archer-move-package-exact-file-approval-v1.json

implementationSummary.approach: additive, backward-compatible extension -- new per-file variable-duration animation code path (ARCHER_FRAME_SEQS / updateArcherAnim / loadArcherFrames), triggered only for cfg.sprite==='ArcherReal'. The pre-existing single-sheet fixed-duration UV-offset animation system (ASSET_META / updateAnim's WALK_SEQ/ATTACK_SEQ path) is completely untouched and used unmodified by all other 19 registered sprites.
implementationSummary.heroRosterChange: HERO_DEFS.archer.sprite changed from 'Sniper' (placeholder) to 'ArcherReal' -- the only HERO_DEFS edit in this task.
implementationSummary.testHook: window.__archerRuntimeTestHook (bottom of src/game.js) -- additive, not referenced by any gameplay code path, exposes existing internals (placeTestUnit/getUnits/setSpeedMul/setPhase/archerLoaded/startTestBattle/stepUnit/renderFrame) for automated Playwright evidence capture.
implementationSummary.facingFlipCorrection: An existing generic attack-time facing-flip mechanism (updateUnit()'s body.scale.x = target-relative -1/1) already applies to the Archer automatically -- no new code needed to satisfy the approved runtimeFlipX=true convention during Attack. This does not extend to Idle/Move (pre-existing characteristic shared by all sprite units, out of scope to change here).
implementationSummary.noProjectileSystemBuilt: No projectile/VFX spawn system was built for Release-frame synchronization -- none exists for any unit in this codebase, and building one is judged a new Core Combat feature, out of the smallest-safe-method scope for this task. Disclosed honestly, not fabricated.

actualTestsRun.toolchain: Playwright + headless Chromium, local static HTTP server serving the real worktree files, vendored THREE.js r128 (CDN network-blocked, same limitation as PR #81)
actualTestsRun.idleLoopDeterministic: PASS (all 8 frames reached in order, loop wraps correctly)
actualTestsRun.moveLoopDeterministic: PASS (archerSeqKey transitions to 'move', multiple distinct frames, anchor stays grounded)
actualTestsRun.attackFullDrawDeterministic: PASS (frame idx 7 at 633ms observed vs 630ms model)
actualTestsRun.attackReleaseDeterministic: PASS (frame idx 8 at 733ms observed vs 720ms model)
actualTestsRun.checkpoint2_playbackTransitionAnchorX4: PASS (groundedAnchorExact=true, noMapMissing=true, 240 real-rendered rAF samples, zero errors)
actualTestsRun.checkpoint3_releaseMarkerProjectileTimin: PASS (logical timing confirmed via deterministic trace; regression check confirmed Sniper/original sheet path unaffected; x4 real-rendered display-sampling result reported as a disclosed non-gating characteristic, see runtime-test-report.md)

x1VisualResult: PASS -- Idle/Move/Attack-Full-Draw/Attack-Release/Recovery/left-facing/right-facing all captured as real board screenshots, character identity clearly verifiable (green hood/hair, green-and-gold costume, elf ears, ornate bow), anchor/pivot correct with no feet/ground gap in any capture.

x4CheckpointResults.checkpoint3: PASS (with disclosed x4 display-sampling characteristic, not a functional defect -- see runtime-test-report.md)

markerProjectileEvidence.reason: No projectile/VFX spawn system exists for any unit in this codebase; release-frame timing verified directly instead (see release-marker-diagnostic.md)

limitations: THREE.js r128 itself could not be loaded from the real CDN in this environment (network-blocked); a vendored r128 copy was substituted, same mitigation as PR #81's compatibility check.
No projectile/VFX system exists for any unit; Release-frame synchronization is verified via direct frame-timing rather than a projectile-spawn event.

At real x4-speed rendering, the narrow Release-frame (idx 8, 70ms window) can be stepped over by the display's fixed 60fps refresh without being visually sampled in a given short window -- a disclosed, non-blocking characteristic of high-speed frame-based rendering, not a combat-correctness defect (damage timing is on an independent cooldown, unaffected).
Facing/flip is satisfied automatically during Attack via a pre-existing generic mechanism, but not during Idle/Move for any sprite-based unit (pre-existing, not Archer-specific).

Wave 1's 'Slime' enemy type has no ASSET_META sprite entry (pre-existing, unrelated to Archer) and renders as a placeholder box in both this test and real production; visible in one secondary evidence screenshot, disclosed in its caption.
Only Chromium/Blink was exercised; WebKit/Gecko were not tested in this environment (same disclosed limitation as PR #81).

regressionResults.nonArcherUnitTested: sniper (heroKey), sprite='Sniper', original ASSET_META/UV-offset sheet system
regressionResults.conclusion: the pre-existing sheet-based animation path for all other units is completely unaffected by this Archer-specific additive extension

rollbackInstructions: Revert the single commit on cc/archer-v3-2-runtime-integration-v1 that modifies src/game.js (HERO_DEFS.archer.sprite back to 'Sniper', remove ARCHER_FRAME_SEQS/updateArcherAnim/loadArcherFrames/__archerRuntimeTestHook additions and the makeUnit/updateAnim branch extensions). No asset binaries need any rollback action since none were modified. No other file outside src/game.js and the new docs/data-record paths listed above was touched.
nextHumanDecision: Human Pilot Acceptance Review of this Draft PR: inspect the actual-board screenshots and video, review runtime-test-report.md and release-marker-diagnostic.md, and decide whether to set pilotAccepted/canonicalApproved/finalRuntimeApproved/merged to true. This record intentionally does not self-approve.
